findings_none: All 109 deleted lines across the 10 edited profiles are motivational or duplicative narration; every frontmatter block is hash-verified byte-identical, every machine-pinned needle survives, both required validators are green at HEAD, and the pre-existing opencode/kimi failures are reproduced byte-equivalent with zero contribution from this diff.

## Review narrative

Candidate isolation. The review context (discovery phase, empty validation_obligations, empty prior_findings, claim_root_base a94ea0b7) was read first. The n7 leg commit is dcc6983e (parent d65684c2, the lane-group stub off run base 1491c7e5). Its diff touches exactly 10 of the 11 agents/*.md files plus the node's own barrier-exempt evidence file `kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/n7-role-agents-trim.md` — nothing else. Three cross-checks: (1) `git diff 1491c7e5..HEAD -- agents/` is byte-identical to `git diff dcc6983e^..dcc6983e -- agents/` (no other lane touched agents/); (2) the stub commit touched zero agents/ files; (3) `agents/knowledge-lookup.md` is byte-identical between run base and HEAD, confirming n7's 10-edited/1-untouched report. The agents/ portion is 109 deletions, 0 insertions, matching n7's table exactly (6/2/2/6/2/2/2/83/2/2).

Diff walk of all 11 files. build-error-resolver.md: opening identity sentence ("You are an expert build error resolution specialist...") plus a trailing `---` + "**Remember**: Fix the error..." aside — both pure narration; the Core Responsibilities and Evidence Contract sections are untouched context. code-architect.md and code-explorer.md: one opening identity sentence each, duplicating the frontmatter `description:`. doc-updater.md: identity sentence plus the "**Remember**: Documentation that doesn't match reality..." aside. implementer.md: the "You are the **implementer**:..." line only; the retained `## Your Role` paragraph (line 28 of agents/implementer.md) still carries the full operational definition including `non_tdd_reason`, `verification_tier`, and the `regression-green`/`build-green`/`smoke-integration` triple. issue-scout.md: identity sentence only; its restated constraints survive in the frontmatter description ("Read-only backlog-clustering agent... recommends one same-scope bundle"), the managed-agent note block, and the `## Hard Boundaries` section (no claim/write/author/close/dispatch). metric-optimizer.md, synthesizer.md, tdd-guide.md: one identity line each; each `## Your Role` retains the full role definition. planner.md: identity line, the entire 78-line `## Worked Example: Adding Stripe Subscriptions` section, and the "**Remember**: A great plan..." aside; the `## Plan Format` template the example instantiated survives in full (Overview/Requirements/Architecture Changes/Implementation Steps/Testing Strategy/Risks and Mitigations/Success Criteria, lines 62-106). knowledge-lookup.md: zero diff, matching n7's declined-cut rationale. No deleted line carries role semantics not restated elsewhere, an evidence/verdict contract line, a pinned needle, or frontmatter.

Frontmatter byte-identity. For all 11 files I extracted the frontmatter block (byte 0 through the closing `---`) at 1491c7e5 and at HEAD and compared sha256 hashes: all 11 SAME. Every diff hunk begins at or after old-file line 23, below each frontmatter close.

Pinned-needle survival. Enumerated every pin landing in the write set: `scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` lines 722-724 pin `verification_tier` + `smoke-integration` into agents/implementer.md and `evidence block contains BOTH literal tokens` into agents/tdd-guide.md — all three verified present at HEAD by direct grep (implementer lines 28/58/60/74; tdd-guide line 116). `scripts/validate-vendored-agents.js` `checkFutureAgentWall` (lines 119-125) needles verified per kind: SELF-WRITE + evidence-binding present in all six write-kind profiles (build-error-resolver, doc-updater, implementer, metric-optimizer, synthesizer, tdd-guide); RETURN + record-evidence present in all five read-kind profiles (code-architect, code-explorer, issue-scout, knowledge-lookup, planner). `## Prompt Defense Baseline` present exactly once in each of the 11 files. A repo-wide grep of all edition scripts (scripts/, plus the codex, gitlab, and gitea validator trees) for distinctive fragments of every deleted line (Worked Example, Stripe, the identity-sentence phrases, the Remember asides) found zero matches — no pin file outside the write set needed narrowing. The other three edition contract validators pin only the separately maintained `.toml` mirrors (`verification_tier` in implementer.toml etc.) and `agents/workflow-planner.md`, all untouched; `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` and `scripts/test-route-reachability.js` reference agents/ paths only as barrier fixtures and workflow-planner pins, never asserting content of the 11 profiles.

Re-run of n7's targeted checks at candidate HEAD. `node scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` exited 0 with "Workflow contract validation passed". `node scripts/validate-vendored-agents.js` exited 0 with "Vendored agent validation passed for 16 agents at 922d2d8f8b64f4e50936e24465cb3bcac81ac0e1". Both match n7's recorded before/after outputs verbatim.

opencode/kimi pre-existing red. `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` at HEAD: exit 1, single failure "FAIL: H1 (#F3): hookPath resolves a hook via the plugin-sibling ../hooks candidate when the project + config dir have none — got null", 385 passed / 1 failed — byte-equivalent to n7's recorded before AND after lines. `node scripts/test-kimi-edition.js` at HEAD: uncaught ENOENT on `hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh` from sync-kimi-edition.js, matching n7's record. Both root-cause to the Phase-C advisory-hook retirement present at run base 1491c7e5, outside n7's write set and already seeded as a run gap. Contribution analysis: both suites recompute their expected agent output live from the same canonical agents/*.md they test against (test-opencode-edition.js line 158 `sync.renderAgent(read('agents/'+name+'.md'))`; test-kimi-edition.js analogous), so a canonical-side deletion cannot desynchronize those comparisons — and indeed every agent-rendering test is inside the 385 passing. The n7 agents/*.md diff contributes nothing to either failure.

Pre-existing observation (non-blocking, not a finding): n7 correctly flagged the pre-existing synthesizer.md `merged_sha` vs ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY `merge_outcome` token-name mismatch as out of scope and left it untouched; it predates this candidate and is orchestrator-routed, not a candidate-caused defect.

review_conclusion: The n7-role-agents-trim diff is a strictly deletion-only narration trim of 10 of the 11 role profiles with knowledge-lookup untouched; every frontmatter block is hash-verified identical, every machine-pinned needle and evidence-contract line survives, both required validators are green at HEAD, the pre-existing opencode and kimi failures are reproduced with zero contribution from this diff, and no edit landed outside the declared write set, so the gate passes with zero findings.
